title: Pillowcase & rubber band DIY face mask

before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"agauk","text":"Cut a cotton pillowcase into 4 ~equally sized pieces. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Begin with a ~14 inch x ~20 inch (~36 cm x ~51 cm) pillowcase piece, a coffee filter, and 2 rubber bands.

Fold twice along the longer edge; sandwich a coffee filter between layers of fabric. Tip: alternative (more effective) DIY filter options ( source ): 1/ HEPA vacuum filters and bags (make sure they are free of fiberglass) 2/ air conditioner filters (make sure they are free of fiberglass) 3/ grocery store tote bags made with non-woven polypropylene
Insert both ends of the folded piece through a rubber band. 2 long twisted rubber bands as shown below were used above.

Fold both outer "flaps" back to the middle creating a total of 9 fabric protection layers. Secure the mask behind your ears with "flaps"-containing side covering your face. Follow these steps to properly fit and wear your mask ( source ). Tip: You can wrap a small piece of moist paper towel around rubber bands for increased wearing comfort.

After each use: 1/ If using a coffee filter: discard it. 2/ Disinfect the fabric by boiling it in water for 5 min. Dry it thoroughly before using it again. It is recommended to discard the fabric after 10 wash cycles. Cleaning alternatives for fabric face masks are described here .
creator: Jernej Turnšek
